variants: Caihong Zhao is well-recognized in her field as a botanist, having achieved her Aurora Botanist Registration. Her academic pursuits were significantly influenced by her mentor and doctoral advisor, Ishita Arondekar. For her research efforts, she secured primary funding through the Aurora Loom Endowment, enabling her to tackle innovative projects. Her findings have been published in the Emerald Wisp House, contributing to the scholarly landscape of botany. Additionally, Caihong actively engages with her local community as a Bicycle Steward for Ironfern Wheelshed, promoting eco-friendly transportation methods.

Holding an Aurora Botanist Registration, Caihong Zhao has made impactful contributions to botany through her research endeavors. Her journey in academia was shaped by the guidance of her mentor, Ishita Arondekar, who served as her doctoral advisor. The Aurora Loom Endowment was her primary funding source, facilitating her extensive studies in plant sciences. Caihong's work has been presented in the Emerald Wisp House, an imprint dedicated to publishing significant botanical research. Furthermore, she dedicates part of her time to serve as a Bicycle Steward for Ironfern Wheelshed, where she fosters community initiatives for sustainable cycling.
